title: Cosmic Cliffs — Carina Nebula
desc: Webb’s first look into the Carina Nebula: a coastline of gas and dust seven light-years tall, where hundreds of new stars are igniting.

title: Pillars of Creation
desc: The most famous towers in astronomy, reimagined in Webb’s infrared — newborn stars glow inside columns of gas five light-years tall.

title: Southern Ring Nebula
desc: A dying star’s final act: shells of gas expelled over thousands of years, lit from within by the fading white dwarf at the center.

title: Stephan’s Quintet
desc: Five galaxies locked in a gravitational dance — two of them in mid-collision, sparking waves of new star formation.

title: Webb’s First Deep Field
desc: The deepest infrared view of the universe: thousands of galaxies in a patch of sky the size of a grain of sand at arm’s length.

title: Tarantula Nebula
desc: The most violent star factory in our galactic neighborhood — 161,000 light-years away, home to the most massive stars known.

title: Cartwheel Galaxy
desc: The aftermath of a bullseye: a smaller galaxy punched through this one 400 million years ago, sending rings of star birth outward like ripples.

title: Pillars of Creation (1995–2015)
desc: Hubble’s 2015 return to the Eagle Nebula’s pillars — the image that defined a generation of astronomy.

title: Veil Nebula
desc: Shrapnel from a star that exploded 8,000 years ago, still expanding — a supernova remnant 110 light-years across.

title: Whirlpool Galaxy
desc: M51 and its small companion: the textbook spiral galaxy, its arms compressed into star-forming brilliance by the encounter.

title: Butterfly Nebula
desc: Wings of gas heated to 20,000 degrees, flowing from a dying star at a million kilometers per hour.

title: Hubble Ultra Deep Field
desc: Hubble stared at one dark speck of sky for weeks and found 10,000 galaxies — some of their light older than the Earth itself.

title: Sombrero Galaxy
desc: A galaxy seen edge-on, its dust lane and glowing bulge forming the brim and crown that give M104 its name.

title: Orion Nebula
desc: The nearest great star factory to Earth — visible to your naked eye as the middle “star” in Orion’s sword.
